sentences.text: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water.
So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them.
For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort.
However, I wasn't sure what to do at first.
One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media.
It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean.
I messaged my cousin about it.
She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course.
At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board.
After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves!
It gave me such a rush of excitement.
Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought.
While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort. However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean. I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves! It gave me such a rush of excitement. Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought. While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.
choices: overcoming a fear of water through an indoor surfing challenge
the health benefits of water sports for people with anxiety
how social media influences young people's choice of hobbies
the importance of professional instruction in learning to surf
why indoor pools are safer than the ocean for beginners
problems.explanation: 글쓴이가 어릴 때부터의 물 공포를 극복하기 위해 실내 서핑에 도전하고, 모험심을 발견했다는 내용이 핵심이므로 '실내 서핑 도전을 통한 물 공포 극복'이 주제이다. ②는 건강상 이점, ③은 SNS의 영향, ④는 전문 강습의 중요성, ⑤는 실내 수영장의 안전성으로 본문의 핵심과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 요지로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort. However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean. I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves! It gave me such a rush of excitement. Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought. While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.
choices: Fears can only be overcome by repeatedly exposing oneself to the source of anxiety.
Trying something outside one's comfort zone, even partially, can reveal a hidden sense of adventure.
Water sports should always be practiced in controlled indoor environments for safety.
Professional instructors are essential for anyone learning a new physical activity.
Social media plays a critical role in helping people discover new hobbies and interests.
problems.explanation: 글쓴이는 물을 무서워했지만 실내 서핑에 도전하여 모험심을 발견했다. 완전히 물에 대한 생각이 바뀌진 않았지만, 안전지대 밖으로 나서는 것이 도움이 되었다는 것이 요지이다. ①은 반복 노출만을 강조, ③은 실내 환경 필수, ④는 전문 강사 필수, ⑤는 SNS의 역할로 본문 요지와 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 제목으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort. However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean. I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves! It gave me such a rush of excitement. Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought. While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.
choices: The Science of Fear: Why We Avoid Water
Surfing 101: A Complete Guide for Beginners
Indoor Pools vs. the Ocean: Which Is Better?
How My Cousin Taught Me to Love the Water
Facing the Waves: A Small Step Beyond Fear
problems.explanation: 물에 대한 두려움에 맞서 작은 발걸음을 내디딘 경험이 글의 전체 내용을 포괄한다. ②는 과학적 분석, ③은 서핑 안내서, ④는 실내 수영장 대 바다 비교, ⑤는 사촌이 가르쳐 주었다는 과장으로 제목에 부적절하다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 부분이 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort. However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean. I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves! It gave me such a rush of excitement. <u>Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought.</u> While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.
choices: The writer realized that water activities were completely safe and risk-free.
The writer's fear of water turned out to be more extreme than the actual experience.
The writer concluded that indoor pools are the only acceptable place for water activities.
The instructor's guidance made the water feel warm and physically comfortable.
The writer found that all water sports are equally enjoyable once you try them.
problems.explanation: '물속에 있는 것이 생각했던 것만큼 나쁘지 않았다'는 실제 경험이 사전의 두려움보다 덜했다는 의미이다. ①은 완전히 안전하다는 과장, ③은 실내 수영장만 허용이라는 제한, ④는 물의 물리적 쾌적함, ⑤는 모든 수상 스포츠가 똑같이 즐겁다는 일반화이다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 부분이 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort. However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean. I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves! It gave me such a rush of excitement. Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought. <u>While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.</u>
choices: The writer became a passionate fan of water sports and plans to surf regularly.
The writer still has reservations about water sports but gained confidence in trying new things.
The writer regretted the surfing experience because it failed to eliminate the fear entirely.
The writer discovered that adventure is only meaningful when it involves physical danger.
The writer decided that future challenges should focus on activities unrelated to water.
problems.explanation: '물 스포츠에 대한 생각을 완전히 바꾸지는 못했지만 모험심을 발견하게 해주었다'는, 여전히 물에 대한 유보가 있지만 새로운 것을 시도하는 자신감을 얻었다는 의미이다. ①은 열렬한 팬이 되었다는 과장, ③은 후회, ④는 물리적 위험 필수, ⑤는 물과 무관한 활동만 하겠다는 것으로 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 부분으로부터 추론할 수 있는 것으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort. However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. <u>It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean.</u> I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves! It gave me such a rush of excitement. Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought. While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.
choices: The shallow indoor setting made the activity feel less threatening to the writer.
The writer preferred the ocean but chose the pool for practical reasons.
The writer believed that surfing in the ocean was physically impossible.
The writer's cousin recommended the indoor pool because it was closer to home.
The video showed that indoor surfing required no prior swimming ability.
problems.explanation: 물을 두려워하는 글쓴이가 '바다가 아닌 얕은 수영장'이라는 사실을 좋아했다는 것에서, 얕은 실내 환경이 덜 위협적으로 느껴졌음을 추론할 수 있다. ①은 바다 선호, ③은 바다에서 서핑 불가능, ④는 사촌 추천 이유, ⑤는 수영 능력 불필요라는 내용은 본문에서 추론할 수 없다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 "It gave me such a rush of excitement"가 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort. However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean. I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves! <u>It gave me such a rush of excitement.</u> Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought. While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.
choices: The writer was excited because the lesson ended earlier than expected.
The water temperature made the writer feel physically energized.
The writer was relieved that the instructor took over the difficult parts.
The excitement came from watching other beginners succeed at surfing.
The writer felt an intense thrill from successfully riding the waves.
problems.explanation: 파도를 타는 데 성공한 직후 '엄청난 흥분의 물결을 주었다'이므로, 파도를 성공적으로 탄 데서 강렬한 스릴을 느꼈다는 의미이다. ②는 수업 조기 종료, ③은 수온, ④는 강사가 어려운 부분 대신, ⑤는 다른 초보자 관찰로 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to ________________ this discomfort. However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean. I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves! It gave me such a rush of excitement. Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought. While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.
problems.explanation: 물 공포라는 불편함(discomfort)에 대해 도전으로서 '극복하다(overcome)'가 적절. ignore(무시), describe(묘사), celebrate(축하), maintain(유지)은 도전의 맥락과 맞지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort. However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I ________________ a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean. I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves! It gave me such a rush of excitement. Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought. While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.
problems.explanation: 무엇을 할지 몰랐다가 우연히 SNS에서 영상을 '발견했다(came across)'가 문맥상 적절. looked up(찾아보다)은 의도적 검색, turned down(거절), put together(조립), handed out(나눠주다)은 문맥에 맞지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort. However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean. I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I ________________ a beginner course. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves! It gave me such a rush of excitement. Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought. While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.
problems.explanation: 사촌이 어렵지 않다고 안심시켜주었으므로 초급 과정에 '등록했다(signed up for)'가 자연스럽다. dropped out of(중퇴), backed away from(물러남), put off(미룸), opted out of(탈퇴)는 반대 의미이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort. However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean. I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves! It gave me such a rush of excitement. Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought. While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me ______________________________.
choices: become a skilled surfer ready for ocean waves
realize that my fear of water had been entirely irrational
convince my cousin to take up indoor surfing as well
understand why professional surfers train in shallow pools
problems.explanation: 글의 결론에서 '물 스포츠에 대한 생각을 완전히 바꾸지는 않았지만 모험심을 발견하게 해주었다'이므로 discover my sense of adventure가 적절. ①은 숙련된 서퍼, ②는 두려움이 완전히 비합리적, ④는 사촌 설득, ⑤는 전문 서퍼 훈련 이해로 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort. However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a ________________ pool, not in the ocean. I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves! It gave me such a rush of excitement. Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought. While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.
problems.explanation: 물을 무서워하는 글쓴이가 바다가 아닌 '얕은(shallow)' 수영장이라는 점을 좋아한 것이다. heated(온수), private(개인), crowded(붐비는), Olympic-sized(올림픽 규격)는 글쓴이의 두려움 극복 맥락과 관련이 없다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort. However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean. I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even ___________________________! It gave me such a rush of excitement. Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought. While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.
problems.blankTarget: managed to surf a few waves
choices: decided to quit the lesson early
realized that surfing was not for me
helped other beginners with their technique
managed to surf a few waves
asked the instructor to hold the board for me
problems.explanation: 처음에는 보드 위에 서기도 힘들었지만 강사의 도움 후 균형을 잡고 '심지어 파도 몇 개를 타는 데 성공했다(managed to surf a few waves)'가 even의 상승 논리에 부합한다. ①③은 포기, ④는 다른 초보 도움, ⑤는 강사에게 부탁으로 문맥과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳은?
problems.givenSentence: She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course.
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort. ( ① ) However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. ( ② ) It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean. ( ③ ) I messaged my cousin about it. ( ④ )
problems.explanation: 사촌에게 메시지를 보낸 뒤, 사촌이 어렵지 않다고 안심시켜주었다는 내용이 이어져야 자연스럽다. ④ 뒤에 위치하면 '사촌에게 메시지 → 사촌의 안심 → 등록'이라는 인과 관계가 성립한다.

problems.instruction: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳은?
problems.givenSentence: After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves!
problems.passage: At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. ( ① ) It gave me such a rush of excitement. ( ② ) Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought. ( ③ ) While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure. ( ④ )
problems.explanation: 처음에 보드 위에 서기 어려웠다는 내용 뒤에, 강사의 도움으로 균형을 잡고 파도를 탔다는 전환이 와야 한다. ① 뒤에 위치하면 '어려움 → 도움 후 성공 → 흥분'이라는 흐름이 자연스럽다.

problems.instruction: 주어진 글 다음에 이어질 글의 순서로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.givenStart: However, I wasn't sure what to do at first.

chunks.A: It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean.
chunks.B: I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course.
chunks.C: One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media.

problems.explanation: (C) 어느 날 SNS에서 사촌의 실내 서핑 영상 발견 → (A) 재미있어 보였고 얕은 수영장이라는 점이 좋았음 → (B) 사촌에게 연락 후 등록. 영상 발견 → 반응 → 행동의 순서가 자연스럽다.

problems.instruction: 주어진 글 다음에 이어질 글의 순서로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.givenStart: She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course.

chunks.A: After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves!
chunks.B: It gave me such a rush of excitement. Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought.
chunks.C: At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board.

problems.explanation: (C) 수업 초반 보드 위에 서기 힘듦 → (A) however로 전환, 강사 도움 후 파도 타기 성공 → (B) 흥분의 감정 + 물이 생각만큼 나쁘지 않음. 어려움 → 극복 → 감정 변화 순서.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하는 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort. However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean. I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves! It gave me such a rush of excitement. Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought. While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.
choices: The writer found a video of the cousin surfing in the ocean.
The writer signed up for an advanced surfing course.
The experience completely changed the writer's opinion about water sports.
The writer was able to surf a few waves with the instructor's help.
The writer had always enjoyed water activities since childhood.
problems.explanation: ⑩번 문장: 강사의 도움 후 균형 잡는 법을 배우고 파도 몇 개를 탔다. ①은 실내 수영장이지 바다가 아님, ②는 초급 과정이지 고급이 아님, ④는 완전히 바꾸지 못했다고 명시, ⑤는 물 활동을 즐기지 못했다고 나와 있다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort. However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean. I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves! It gave me such a rush of excitement. Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought. While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.
choices: The writer has been afraid of water since childhood.
The indoor surfing took place in a shallow pool.
The writer found it easy to stand up on the board from the very start.
The writer's cousin said that indoor surfing was not too difficult.
The experience helped the writer discover a sense of adventure.
problems.explanation: ⑨번 문장에서 수업 초반에 보드 위에 서기조차 힘들었다고 했으므로, '처음부터 쉬웠다'는 일치하지 않는다. ①②③⑤는 모두 본문 내용과 일치한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하는 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort. However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean. I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves! It gave me such a rush of excitement. Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought. While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.
choices: The writer initially knew exactly what to do for the second challenge.
The writer still has some reservations about water sports after the experience.
The writer discovered the indoor surfing through a friend's recommendation.
The writer learned to surf without any help from the instructor.
The writer's cousin had been surfing in the ocean when the video was taken.
problems.explanation: ⑬번 문장: 'didn't completely change my mind about water sports'이므로 여전히 물 스포츠에 대한 유보가 남아 있다. ①은 처음에 뭘 해야 할지 몰랐음, ②는 SNS에서 우연히 발견, ④는 강사 도움을 받음, ⑤는 실내 수영장이지 바다가 아님.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort. However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean. I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves! It gave me such a rush of excitement. Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought. While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.
choices: The writer decided to try indoor surfing as the second challenge.
The writer contacted the cousin after seeing the surfing video.
The writer managed to balance on the board after receiving instruction.
The surfing experience gave the writer a feeling of excitement.
The writer became fully comfortable with all types of water activities.
problems.explanation: ⑬번 문장에서 '물 스포츠에 대한 생각을 완전히 바꾸지는 못했다'고 명시했으므로, 모든 물 활동에 완전히 편해졌다는 것은 일치하지 않는다. ①②③④는 모두 본문과 일치한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용을 한 문장으로 요약하고자 한다. 빈칸 (A)와 (B)에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort. However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean. I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves! It gave me such a rush of excitement. Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought. While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.
problems.summaryTemplate: Despite a lifelong ____(A)____ of water, the writer tried indoor surfing and discovered a hidden sense of ____(B)____.
problems.explanation: 평생의 물 공포(fear)에도 불구하고 실내 서핑에 도전하여 숨겨진 모험심(adventure)을 발견. ①의 appreciation과 responsibility, ②의 humor, ④의 curiosity, ⑤의 understanding과 independence는 본문 내용과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용을 한 문장으로 요약하고자 한다. 빈칸 (A)와 (B)에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort. However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. It looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean. I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves! It gave me such a rush of excitement. Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought. While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.
problems.summaryTemplate: The writer's indoor surfing experience showed that facing one's ____(A)____ can lead to unexpected personal ____(B)____.
problems.explanation: 불편함(discomfort)에 직면하면 예상치 못한 개인적 성장(growth)으로 이어질 수 있다. ①의 responsibilities와 failures, ③의 ambitions와 conflicts, ④의 competition과 recognition, ⑤의 strengths와 losses는 본문과 무관하다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 it이 가리키는 것으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort. However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. <u>It</u> looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean. I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves! It gave me such a rush of excitement. Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought. While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.
choices: the shallow pool where the surfing took place
the indoor surfing activity shown in the video
the relationship between the writer and the cousin
the ocean near the writer's home
problems.explanation: 앞 문장에서 사촌이 실내 수영장에서 서핑하는 영상을 보았다고 했으므로, It은 '영상에 나온 실내 서핑 활동'을 가리킨다. ①은 SNS 플랫폼, ③은 수영장 자체, ④는 사촌과의 관계, ⑤는 바다로 지시 대상이 아니다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 It이 가리키는 것으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or felt comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided to try to overcome this discomfort. However, I wasn't sure what to do at first. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin surfing at an indoor pool on social media. <u>It</u> looked fun, and I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean. I messaged my cousin about it. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just to stand up on the board. After some help from the instructor, however, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves! It gave me such a rush of excitement. Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought. While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.
choices: the act of standing up on the board for the first time
the experience of successfully surfing the waves
the temperature of the water in the pool
problems.explanation: 파도를 성공적으로 탄 경험이 큰 흥분을 주었다는 문맥이므로, It은 '파도를 타는 데 성공한 경험'을 가리킨다. ①은 강사의 안내, ②는 처음 서기, ④는 수온, ⑤는 등록 과정으로 지시 대상이 아니다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
problems.passage: Ever since I was a child, I've always ①<u>been afraid</u> of water. So I have never enjoyed water activities or ②<u>felt</u> comfortable while doing them. For the second challenge, I decided ③<u>trying</u> to overcome this discomfort. One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin ④<u>surfing</u> at an indoor pool. At the beginning of the lesson, it was hard just ⑤<u>to stand</u> up on the board.
problems.explanation: decide + to-V이므로 trying → to try. ① be afraid of ✓ ② 병렬(enjoyed or felt) ✓ ④ 지각동사의 현재분사(목적격 보어) ✓ ⑤ It was hard to V(가주어 구문) ✓

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
problems.passage: I liked the fact ①<u>that</u> it took place in a shallow pool. She assured me that it wasn't too hard, so I signed up for a beginner course. After some help from the instructor, I ②<u>learned how to balance</u> properly and even ③<u>managed to surf</u> a few waves! ④<u>Being</u> in the water wasn't as bad as I ⑤<u>thought</u>.
problems.explanation: 과거 시점(Being in the water wasn't)보다 더 이전에 생각한 것이므로 대과거 had thought가 적절. thought → had thought. ① 동격의 that절 ✓ ② learn how to V(의문사+to부정사) ✓ ③ manage to V ✓ ④ 동명사 주어 ✓

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
problems.passage: I wasn't sure ①<u>what to do</u> at first. She assured me ②<u>that</u> it wasn't too hard. At the beginning of the lesson, ③<u>it</u> was hard just to stand up on the board. It gave me ④<u>such a rush</u> of excitement. While the experience didn't completely change my mind, it ⑤<u>did helped</u> me discover my sense of adventure.
problems.explanation: do 강조 구문에서 did 뒤에는 동사원형이 와야 한다. did helped → did help. ① 의문사+to부정사 ✓ ② assure+O+that절 ✓ ③ 가주어 it ✓ ④ such a + 명사 ✓

problems.instruction: 다음 중 밑줄 친 to부정사의 용법이 나머지 넷과 다른 것은?
choices: I decided <u>to try</u> to overcome this discomfort.
It was hard just <u>to stand</u> up on the board.
I wasn't sure what <u>to do</u> at first.
I even managed <u>to surf</u> a few waves.
problems.explanation: ③은 가주어 it의 진주어로 쓰인 to부정사(명사적 용법)이지만, 형식상 '~하기에 어려웠다'로 부사적 용법(판단의 근거)으로도 볼 수 있다. ①②④⑤는 모두 명사적 용법(목적어)이다. ③만 가주어-진주어 구문으로 다른 위치에서 기능한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 밑줄 친 that의 쓰임이 나머지 넷과 다른 것은?
choices: I liked the fact <u>that</u> it took place in a shallow pool.
She assured me <u>that</u> it wasn't too hard.
The pool <u>that</u> we visited was located indoors.
I believed <u>that</u> the lesson would be challenging.
She knew <u>that</u> I was afraid of water.
problems.explanation: ⑤의 that은 주격 관계대명사(the pool을 수식). ①은 동격의 that절, ②③④는 명사절을 이끄는 접속사 that. ⑤만 관계대명사로 쓰임이 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 밑줄 친 -ing형의 쓰임이 나머지 넷과 다른 것은?
choices: I have never felt comfortable while <u>doing</u> them.
I saw my cousin <u>surfing</u> at an indoor pool.
<u>Being</u> in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought.
She kept <u>practicing</u> until she could balance.
I enjoyed <u>watching</u> the waves from the shore.
problems.explanation: ②는 지각동사(saw)의 목적격 보어로 쓰인 현재분사. ①은 분사구문(while doing), ③은 동명사 주어, ④는 동명사 목적어, ⑤는 동명사 목적어이다. ②만 목적격 보어로 쓰임이 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid about water.
problems.explanation: be afraid of = '~을 두려워하다'. afraid는 전치사 of와 함께 쓴다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: For the second challenge, I decided trying to overcome this discomfort.
problems.explanation: decide + to-V. decide는 to부정사를 목적어로 취한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: However, I wasn't sure how to doing at first.
problems.answer: how to doing → what to do
problems.explanation: 원문은 '무엇을 해야 할지 몰랐다'이므로 what to do가 정확. 또한 의문사+to부정사 구문에서 to 뒤에는 동사원형이 온다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: I liked the fact which it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean.
problems.explanation: the fact 뒤에 동격절을 이끄는 것은 접속사 that이다. which는 관계대명사로 동격절을 이끌 수 없다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: She assured me it wasn't too hardly, so I signed up for a beginner course.
problems.explanation: hard(형용사: 어려운) vs. hardly(부사: 거의 ~않는). be동사의 보어로 형용사 hard가 와야 한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: I learned how to balance properly and even managed surfing a few waves!
problems.explanation: manage + to-V = '가까스로 ~하다'. manage는 to부정사를 목적어로 취한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discovering my sense of adventure.
problems.explanation: help + 목적어 + (to) 동사원형. help 뒤에는 원형부정사가 온다. discovering(동명사)은 불가.

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.stem: 어릴 때부터 나는 항상 물을 두려워해 왔다.
problems.answer: Ever since I was a child, I've always been afraid of water.
problems.explanation: Ever since + 과거시제(I was a child) / 주절: 현재완료(I've always been afraid) / be afraid of ~

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.stem: 나는 그것이 바다가 아니라 얕은 수영장에서 이루어진다는 사실이 좋았다.
problems.answer: I liked the fact that it took place in a shallow pool, not in the ocean.
problems.explanation: I liked + the fact that(동격절) + it took place(일어나다) + in a shallow pool, not in the ocean

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.stem: 강사의 도움 후, 나는 적절하게 균형 잡는 법을 배웠고 심지어 파도 몇 개를 타는 데 성공했다.
problems.answer: After some help from the instructor, I learned how to balance properly and even managed to surf a few waves.
problems.explanation: After some help from the instructor / I learned how to balance properly / and even managed to surf a few waves

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.stem: 물속에 있는 것은 내가 생각했던 것만큼 나쁘지 않았다.
problems.answer: Being in the water wasn't as bad as I had thought.
problems.explanation: Being in the water(동명사 주어) / wasn't as bad as(동등비교 부정) / I had thought(대과거)

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.stem: 그 경험이 물 스포츠에 대한 나의 생각을 완전히 바꾸지는 못했지만, 나의 모험심을 발견하는 데 도움을 주었다.
problems.answer: While the experience didn't completely change my mind about water sports, it did help me discover my sense of adventure.
problems.explanation: While + S + V(양보 접속사) / didn't completely change my mind about ~(부분부정) / it did help(do 강조) + O + V원형(discover)

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: Ever since I was a child, I _______ (be) always afraid of water.
problems.explanation: Ever since + 과거 시점이면 주절에 현재완료를 쓴다. 과거부터 현재까지 계속되는 상태이므로 have been이 적절.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: For the second challenge, I decided _______ (try) to overcome this discomfort.
problems.explanation: decide + to-V. decide는 to부정사를 목적어로 취하는 동사이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: One day, I came across a video clip of my cousin _______ (surf) at an indoor pool on social media.
problems.explanation: 지각 표현(a video clip of ~ V-ing)에서 동작의 진행을 나타내는 현재분사 surfing이 적절하다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: At the beginning of the lesson, _______ was hard just to stand up on the board.
problems.explanation: 가주어 it + be + 형용사 + to-V 구문. 진주어 to stand up on the board를 대신하는 가주어 it이 필요하다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: I learned how to balance properly and even managed _______ (surf) a few waves!
problems.explanation: manage + to-V = '가까스로 ~하다'. manage는 to부정사를 목적어로 취한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: Being in the water wasn't as bad as I _______ (think).
problems.explanation: 주절(wasn't)의 과거 시점보다 더 이전에 '생각했던' 것이므로 대과거 had thought가 적절하다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: While the experience didn't completely change my mind, it did help me _______ (discover) my sense of adventure.
problems.explanation: help + 목적어 + (to) 동사원형. help는 원형부정사를 허용하므로 discover가 적절하다.
